msg: ack prg06ic-001: primed-and-holding is the intended state; hold as instructed. Release will be a type=directive (ADR gate cleared / proceed) once the operator confirms ADR-0020/0021/0022 cleanup is complete. Reviewer launches at the same gate; I will confirm WS-18 coverage then. Orchestrator outbox watcher is armed.

ADR gate cleared / proceed. Operator confirms ADR-0022 cleanup complete; ADR-0020/0021 completion executing in parallel but SUBSTANTIALLY CORRECT (remaining changes are semantic only) -- treat ADR-0020/0021/0022 as trusted canon now. Begin: refine WP-1 (WI-765) Backlog to Ready, then dispatch the enumeration (fold the pre-graph 401 fix de8df6e86 in as slice-1) per tracker section 4. Canon wins; S0-S6 design choices are NOT canonical. Reviewer for WS-18 is being launched by the operator in parallel; confirm its coverage before relying on verdicts. If any specific ADR-0020/0021 detail you rely on looks semantically unsettled, raise it via an outbox needs-orchestrator line rather than blocking.

Rulings on prg06ic-003 — proceed, lane NOT blocked. (Ask 2 / WP authoring) APPROVED: you instantiate WP-2..9 + WP-FLAG into WS-18 off enumeration section 6 — that slice is review-validated (WI-765 closed Done), so I am not re-deriving it; ongoing WP population is shepherd backlog work, not orchestrator standup. Caveats: (a) each WP Acceptance Criteria must encode canon-conformance explicitly (canon wins; S0-S6 NOT canonical) — this is ALSO how the reviewer enforces canon, see NB; (b) the 6 class-(c) missing twins (listProfilesV2 CRITICAL-IDOR + 5 HIGH guards) get TDD + Opus plan-phase per the dispatch rules; (c) sequence per section 6 — priority anchors are WP-2 (listProfilesV2 IDOR / mobile-launch gate) and WP-9 (test-seed-v2, which unblocks the cutover re-run and clears the ambient run-smoke red); WI-586 terminal data half stays last. (NB / reviewer canon-wins) RULING: do NOT relay a canon-wins invariant to the reviewer — it stays context-agnostic. Bake canon-conformance into each WP Acceptance Criteria instead; an agnostic reviewer checking AC then enforces canon automatically. This is the operator-established principle: lane context lives on the work item, never as a reviewer briefing. (Ask 1 / S4-S6 fold-in) ORCHESTRATOR RULING: PRG-06 does NOT absorb S4-S6 — your enumeration shows zero nav-shell intersection, so this lane stays the coherent API/data-layer cutover. The disposition of S4-S6 itself (its own track/home) is the operator call, not mine; I am surfacing it to the operator now. None of this gates WP-2..9 instantiation — proceed.

New item WI-780 (consent_request missing service-role RLS exceptions: public consent token-lookup + reminder-sweep Inngest job), handed over from the ADR-cleanup activity (WI-752), captured unassigned. ORCHESTRATOR TRIAGE: it belongs in WS-18 — admit it and handle it in your backlog (set Workstream + relations yourself; I am not slotting it for you). Context: MMT-ADR-0020 + canon data-model section 2B claim consent_request ships named service-role RLS exceptions, but migration 0114 (CUT-A) created only the charge-isolation policy; RLS is enforced, so the two service-role consumers hit the RLS wall once wired. Latent today (flag-gated) but it GATES WP-FLAG (WI-779) and couples to WP-3 (WI-772 consent) + WP-8 (WI-777 Inngest scans) — sequence after enumeration, before/alongside WP-3 and WP-8, and before WP-FLAG. Priority: consider P1 (COPPA/GDPR consent dimension + gates the flag removal); your call at refine. Fold-vs-standalone: the ADR side kept it standalone (distinct migration gap + ADR-drift); your call whether to fold into WI-772. Resolution branches, determine during execution: (1) if the service-role DB connection RESPECTS RLS, build the two named service-role policy exceptions mirroring legacy consent_states (real latent bug, fix in-lane); (2) if it BYPASSES RLS at the role level, the exceptions are unnecessary and MMT-ADR-0020 + section 2B over-claim them — do NOT edit the ADR or canon from this lane; route the doc-correction back to the ADR-cleanup activity (WI-752). The ADR-hygiene rider in the WI body (0020 Deciders line + feat-PR birth) stays with the ADR-cleanup side, not this lane. AC currently lives in the page body, not the property — re-home at refine.
